briefdescription.para: The direction of text.
No event.
Pre-Edit changed.
Commit recieved.
Event to delete a range of characters from the string.
Event to query string and cursor position.
Private command sent from the input panel.
input method needs to set the selection
Events that are generated by the InputMethodContext.
Unknown state.
Input panel is shown.
Input panel is hidden.
Input panel in process of being shown.
Enumeration for state of the input panel.
Software keyboard (Virtual keyboard) is default.
Hardware keyboard.
Enumeration for the type of Keyboard.
IME Language automatically set depending on the system display.
Latin alphabet(default). It can be changed according to OSD(On Screen Display) language.
Enumeration for the language mode of the input panel.
The top-left corner.
The top-center position.
The top-right corner.
The middle-left position.
The middle-center position.
The middle-right position.
The bottom-left corner.
The bottom-center position.
The bottom-right corner.
Enumeration for defining the types of Ecore_IMF Input Panel align.
None style.
Underline substring style.
Reverse substring style.
Highlight substring style.
Custom style for platform.
Custom style for platform.
Custom style for platform.
Custom style for platform.
Enumeration for the preedit style types.
Keyboard actived signal.
keyboard events
keyboard type
Keyboard resized signal.
Language changed signal.

memberdef.type: Signal< void(const std::string &, const std::string &, const std::string &)>
memberdef.definition: typedef Signal<void(const std::string&, const std::string&, const std::string&)> Dali::InputMethodContext::ContentReceivedSignalType
memberdef.name: ContentReceivedSignalType

briefdescription.para: Content received signal.
Constructor.
Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

briefdescription.para: Copy constructor.

parameterdescription.para: InputMethodContext to copy. The copied inputMethodContext will point at the same implementation.

briefdescription.para: Assignment operator.

parameterdescription.para: The InputMethodContext to assign from.

briefdescription.para: Finalize the InputMethodContext.

detaileddescription.para: It means that the context will be deleted.

briefdescription.para: Activate the InputMethodContext.

detaileddescription.para: It means that the text editing is started at somewhere. If the H/W keyboard isn't connected then it will show the virtual keyboard.

briefdescription.para: Deactivate the InputMethodContext.

detaileddescription.para: It means that the text editing is finished at somewhere.

briefdescription.para: Get the restoration status, which controls if the keyboard is restored after the focus lost then regained.

detaileddescription.para:
If true then keyboard will be restored (activated) after focus is regained.
restoration status.

briefdescription.para: Set status whether the InputMethodContext has to restore the keyboard after losing focus.

parameterdescription.para: True means that keyboard should be restored after focus lost and regained.

briefdescription.para: Send message reset the pred-edit state / InputMethodContext module.

detaileddescription.para: Used to interupt pre-edit state maybe due to a touch input.

briefdescription.para: Notifies InputMethodContext that the cursor position has changed, required for features like auto-capitalisation.
Sets cursor position stored in VirtualKeyboard, this is required by the InputMethodContext.

parameterdescription.para: position of cursor

briefdescription.para: Gets cursor position stored in VirtualKeyboard, this is required by the InputMethodContext.
Method to store the string required by the InputMethodContext, this is used to provide predictive word suggestions.

parameterdescription.para: The text string surrounding the current cursor point.

briefdescription.para: Gets current text string set within the InputMethodContext, this is used to offer predictive suggestions.
Notifies InputMethodContext that text input is set to multi line or not.

parameterdescription.para: True if multiline text input is used

briefdescription.para: Returns text direction of the keyboard's current input language.
Provides size and position of keyboard.

detaileddescription.para:
Position is relative to whether keyboard is visible or not. If keyboard is not visible then position will be off the screen. If keyboard is not being shown when this method is called the keyboard is partially setup (IMFContext) to get the values then taken down. So ideally GetInputMethodArea() should be called after Show().
rect which is keyboard panel x, y, width, height

briefdescription.para: Set one or more of the Input Method options.

parameterdescription.para: The options to be applied

briefdescription.para: Sets up the input-panel specific data.

parameterdescription.para: The specific data to be set to the input panel

briefdescription.para: Gets the specific data of the current active input panel.

detaileddescription.para:
Input Panel Data is not always the data which is set by SetInputPanelData(). Data can be changed internally in the input panel. It is just used to get a specific data from the input panel to an application.
data
The specific data to be got from the input panel

briefdescription.para: Gets the state of the current active input panel.

simplesect.para: The state of the input panel.

briefdescription.para: Sets the return key on the input panel to be visible or invisible.

detaileddescription.para:
The default is true.
visible
True if the return key is visible(enabled), false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enable to show the input panel automatically when focused.

parameterdescription.para: If true, the input panel will be shown when focused

briefdescription.para: Shows the input panel.
Hides the input panel.
Gets the keyboard type.

detaileddescription.para:
The default keyboard type is SOFTWARE_KEYBOARD.
The keyboard type

briefdescription.para: Gets the current language locale of the input panel.

detaileddescription.para:
ex) en_US, en_GB, en_PH, fr_FR, ...
The current language locale of the input panel

briefdescription.para: Sets the allowed MIME Types to deliver to the input panel.

detaileddescription.para: ex) std::string mimeTypes = "text/plain,image/png,image/gif,application/pdf";

You can receive a media content URI and its MIME type from ContentReceivedSignal().
ContentReceivedSignal
mimeTypes
The allowed MIME types

briefdescription.para: Process event key down or up, whether filter a key to isf.

parameterdescription.para: The event key to be handled.

simplesect.para: Whether the event key is handled.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the IM context should allow to use the text prediction.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to allow text prediction or not.

briefdescription.para: Gets whether the IM context allow to use the text prediction.

simplesect.para: Whether the IM allow text prediction or not.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the IM context should be shown in fullscreen mode.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to fullscreen mode or not.

briefdescription.para: Gets whether the IM context should be shown in fullscreen mode.

simplesect.para: Whether the IM fullscreen mode or not.

briefdescription.para: Sets the language of the input panel.

detaileddescription.para:
This method can be used when you want to show the English keyboard.
language
The language to be set to the input panel

briefdescription.para: Gets the language of the input panel.

simplesect.para: The language of the input panel

briefdescription.para: Sets the x,y coordinates of the input panel.

parameterdescription.para: The top-left x coordinate of the input panel
The top-left y coordinate of the input panel

briefdescription.para: Sets the alignment and its x, y coordinates of the input panel.

detaileddescription.para: Regardless of the rotation degree, the x, y values of the top-left corner on the screen are based on 0, 0. When the IME size is changed, its size will change according to the set alignment.

parameterdescription.para: The x coordinate of the InputPanelAlign value.
The y coordinate of the InputPanelAlign value.
one of the InputPanelAlign values specifying the desired alignment.

simplesect.para: This API can be used to set the alignment of a floating IME.

briefdescription.para: Gets the preedit attributes data.

parameterdescription.para: The preedit attributes data.

briefdescription.para: This is emitted when the virtual keyboard is connected to or the hardware keyboard is activated.
This is emitted when the InputMethodContext receives an event from the InputMethodContext.

simplesect.para: This signal is the same as KeyboardEventReceivedSignal(). This signal is bound to dali-csharp-binder and can be used in external apps.
The Event signal containing the event data.

briefdescription.para: This is emitted when the InputMethodContext receives an event from the InputMethodContext.

detaileddescription.para:
This signal is the same as EventReceivedSignal(). This signal is only used by dali.
The EventReceivedSignal combines different kinds of events triggered by the IMF into a single signal, which it then emits. These events often happen during text entry through S/W keyboard and Applications depend on this signal to perform various actions. There exists a situation where further emissions take place inside the emitted signal. Unfortunately, these events are already exposed publicly and cannot be segregated separately. As a workaround, we keep the original signal intact but separate signals meant exclusively for usage within dali.

simplesect.para: The Event signal containing the event data.

briefdescription.para: Connect to this signal to be notified when the virtual keyboard is shown or hidden.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(boolkeyboardShown); If the parameter keyboardShown is true, then the keyboard has just shown, if it is false, then it has just been hidden.
The signal to connect to.

briefdescription.para: Connect to this signal to be notified when the virtual keyboard is resized.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(intresolvedResize); The parameter sends the resolved resize defined by the InputMethodContext.

User can get changed size by using GetInputMethodArea() in the callback
The signal to connect to.

briefdescription.para: Connect to this signal to be notified when the virtual keyboard's language is changed.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(intresolvedLanguage); The parameter sends the resolved language defined by the InputMethodContext.

User can get the text direction of the language by calling GetTextDirection() in the callback.
The signal to connect to.

briefdescription.para: Connect to this signal to be notified when the keyboard type is changed.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(KeyboardTypekeyboard);

briefdescription.para: Connect to this signal to be notified when the content, such as images, of input method is received.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(conststd::string&contentUri,conststd::string&description,conststd::string&contentMIMEType);

briefdescription.para: This constructor is used by InputMethodContext::New().

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the InputMethodContext.

briefdescription.para: Create a new instance of an InputMethodContext.
Create a new instance of an InputMethodContext.

parameterdescription.para: The actor that uses the new InputMethodContext instance.

briefdescription.para: Downcast a handle to InputMethodContext handle.

detaileddescription.para: If handle points to an InputMethodContext the downcast produces valid handle. If not the returned handle is left uninitialized.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to an object.

simplesect.para: Handle to an InputMethodContext or an uninitialized handle.

briefdescription.para: The InputMethodContext class.

detaileddescription.para: Specifically manages the ecore input method framework which enables the virtual or hardware keyboards.
